extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN won the sprint after Quick-Step, Lotto, Jumbo, and FDJ controlled the breakaway, finding the right wheel despite not being fastest in pure speed
tactical_patterns: Four teams (Lotto, Jumbo, Quick-Step, FDJ) shared chase work equally, suggesting pre-race sprint cooperation agreement against Sky's ELIA
GROENEWEGEN prioritized positioning over pure speed on rising finish, beating faster VIVIANI through wheel selection not watts
Breakaway caught with 4km remaining indicates teams allowed minimal safety margin, risking miscalculation for tired sprinters

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: Fifth win of season shows exceptional early form, tactical wheel-finding ability compensating for inferior pure speed
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Fastest in field but lost through positioning errors, still winless at Paris-Nice despite speed advantage
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: neutral: Third place confirms resurgent form but lacks explosive positioning needed on technical sprint finishes
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Fourth on GC via bonus seconds from intermediate sprint reveals GC ambitions beyond expected stage-hunting role
rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: neutral: Attempted breakaway shows willingness to attack early but lacks acceleration to establish winning gaps
